arts.ttl: Arsenal legend reveals what teen sensation Myles Lewis-Skelly is like behind closed doors
arts.sum: Arsenal legend David Seaman has described Myles Lewis-Skelly as a "quiet lad" off the pitch despite the youngster's fiery introduction to Premier League football against Manchester City . Arteta clearly has faith in his young talents and offered praise when questioned on Lewis-Skelly's qualities and his debut performance against Manchester City.

arts.ttl: Arsenal 25-26 Home, Away & Third Kits Info Leaked
arts.sum: The Arsenal 2025-26 kits will be worth the wait.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta sticks to his word after hinting at Arsenal's plan for Ethan Nwaneri
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta heaped praise on Ethan Nwaneri after his brace against Bolton Wanderers in midweek but called for patience over his progress - with the Spaniard opting to name teenager on the bench against Leicester

arts.ttl: and Arsenal still 'front of the queue'
arts.sum: Benjamin Sesko has ended his Bundesliga scoring drought - and Arsenal still plan to sign him, according to a report. Sesko ended his drought by scoring a brace with two very different but equally impressive goals.

arts.ttl: Arsenal to face SHOCK rival for Nico Williams transfer
arts.sum: Arsenal could face shock interest from a Premier League rival to sign Nico Williams next summer, with Manchester United reportedly entering the race for the Athletic Bilbao winger. Williams was a top target for Arsenal and Barcelona this summer but the winger opted to remain with Athletic Club for another season despite the strong pursuit of some of Europe’s major sides.

arts.ttl: Arsenal vs Leicester LIVE: Score and latest updates as Leandro Trossard doubles Gunners’ lead
arts.sum: The Gunners will go level on points with Premier League title rivals Man City if they can beat the Foxes

arts.ttl: Bukayo Saka could be about to break Premier League record held by Arsenal hero Thierry Henry
arts.sum: That goal means that Saka has provided an assist in every Premier League game so far this season. Arsenal return to the Emirates Stadium in the Premier League this afternoon (3pm) - and the Gunners are heavy favourites as they take on Leicester City.

arts.ttl: Kai Havertz admits he was taken aback by Martin Odegaard's dressing room behaviour
arts.sum: Kai Havertz has heaped praise on Arsenal captain Martin Odegaard but has admitted he was surprised by the Norwegian's behaviour in the Gunners' dressing room

arts.ttl: LIVE: Title-chasing Arsenal host Leicester City
arts.sum: David Raya and Jurrien Timber overcome injury concerns to start for Arsenal - follow for live updates from the Emirates Stadium.

arts.ttl: Arsenal 'willing' to make SHOCK January transfer
arts.sum: Brazilian outlet Bola has claimed that Arsenal are ready to sell Jesus in January, with the forward’s former club Palmeiras keen to sign him for €40m. Palmeiras are understood to see Jesus as a ‘dream’ target due to his links with the club and a transfer would enable the Brazilian giants to have a heavyweight striker in place for their 2025 Club World Cup push.
